title: Restructure the Halden capability note answer-first
guidance: Reward an answer-first structure with clean, well-nested bullets. Penalise keeping the build-up or burying the recommendation.

runner_input.instruction: Restructure the note below so the recommendation leads, then the supporting arguments with their evidence.
runner_input.constraints: Output: markdown — a bold recommendation on the first line, then nested bullets (top-level claims, with evidence indented beneath each).

source_blocks.text: The org chart lists 11 developers, 1 DevOps, 2 product and 3 QA, and no ML or data engineer. AutoSchedule scored 88–95% on a 12,000-SKU test, implying a real ML pipeline. The CTO is praised and an analytics module was pulled forward. Heavy model calls run via a vendor API. Because we can't see ML ownership, we can't yet size the £6–9m above-plan upside. So capability is green-amber: it gates the size, not the existence, of the upside, and the management session must confirm ML ownership.

rubric_notes: Line 1: capability is green-amber, gates the size of the upside; confirm ML ownership.
Nested evidence: product output implies ML; org chart shows no role; vendor-API dependency.
